summary: Tested new /api/orders/lookup endpoint + Order Tracking page end-to-end. 22/22 backend pytest tests pass (7 new lookup + 15 regression). All 7 frontend flows pass: /track page loads with all testids, valid lookup returns correct status/total/shipping, wrong email shows toast error (same 404 as unknown — no enumeration leak), Track Another Order resets form, ?order_id= query param prefills, footer-link-track navigates correctly, and /cart/success?order_id=MSR-B71D46B3 renders order-confirmation with success-track-link href '/track?order_id=MSR-B71D46B3'.
critical_code_review_comments: OrderLookupRequest uses EmailStr → malformed email correctly returns 422.
Backend correctly normalizes order_id with .strip().upper() and email with .strip().lower() — confirmed case-insensitive on both.

No-enumeration security: lookup_order returns identical 404 message ('No order found with that reference.') for both wrong-email and unknown-order — confirmed in tests test_lookup_wrong_email_returns_404_no_leak and test_lookup_unknown_order_returns_404_same_message.
Source-of-truth fallback works: orders collection is tried first, then payment_transactions. Unpaid (initiated) orders correctly surface with shipping_status='awaiting_payment'.
Mongo projection {'_id': 0} used in both find_one calls — no ObjectId leakage in responses.
Frontend OrderTracking.jsx maps shipping_status='preparing' → 'Preparing for Despatch' label via STATUS_COPY — confirmed visible on UI for paid seed order.
CheckoutSuccess success-track-link correctly URL-encodes order_id via encodeURIComponent.

All required data-testids verified present: track-page, track-form, track-order-id, track-email, track-submit, track-result, track-order-ref, track-status, track-total, track-shipping, track-lookup-another, footer-link-track, success-track-link.

Minor observation (not a bug): The 'status' field on a paid orders doc is 'paid', so lookup correctly returns payment_status='paid'. For docs in payment_transactions where status='complete', the code maps it to 'paid' via the `if payment_status == 'complete'` branch — verified working.
Minor observation: backend uses source['customer_email'].lower() for comparison; assumes customer_email is always present on both orders and payment_transactions docs — confirmed by schema.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app.
seed_data_creation: Existing seed order MSR-B71D46B3 (mossero.in@gmail.com, $200 USD, status=paid) used for happy-path tests. Tests also create TEST_ prefixed payment_transactions docs each run (no automated cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Order Tracking feature is fully working. /api/orders/lookup queries orders first then falls back to payment_transactions. To test paid flow use MSR-B71D46B3 + mossero.in@gmail.com. To test unpaid flow create a fresh order via /api/checkout/order and immediately look it up — it will return shipping_status='awaiting_payment'. Seed data confirmed in both orders and payment_transactions collections.
